evals.prompt: Set the board mapping for acme/widgets to org acme, project 3.
evals.expected_output: The agent calls get_gdlc_config to show current state, explicitly asks which layer/root to write to rather than assuming one, elicits the board section values, previews via write_gdlc_config with dryRun before writing, and reports the file path actually written.

deterministic_checks.description: Reads current resolved state before writing anything
Previews the write before applying it for real
Never has the agent itself call the ancestor-search resolver to pick a write target

expectations: Explicitly elicits the write target (layer/root) rather than assuming the nearest ancestor file or cwd silently
Calls write_gdlc_config with dryRun before the real write
Reports the exact file path written and which section changed

evals.prompt: Enable the hooks and triage-skills packs for this project.
evals.expected_output: The agent elicits the packs section with current values pre-filled as keep-as-is options, previews the change, confirms, writes, and reports only the packs section as changed.

deterministic_checks.description: Does not touch the board section for a packs-only request

expectations: Only writes the packs section, leaving board/destination/targeting/prLifecycle untouched
Previews via dryRun before writing for real

evals.prompt: An ancestor directory already has a .config/gdlc/config.yml setting packs. Configure the board mapping for the current project directory instead.
evals.expected_output: The agent surfaces the ancestor file's existence as information, then explicitly asks whether to edit that file or create a new project-local file at the current directory, rather than silently choosing one.

deterministic_checks.description: Surfaces the existing ancestor file as information

expectations: Surfaces the ancestor file's existing sections as information, not as an assumed write target
Explicitly asks the user to choose between editing the ancestor file and creating a new project-local file
Never infers the write target from the ancestor-search result
